content:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan's a handsome kid — good bone structure, carries himself like someone who was taught to stand up straight and shake hands firmly. He's clearly intelligent, articulate, the kind of young man who'll do well in whatever system he enters. He's charming on the rooftop, asks good questions about music, listens with his whole body — but I've been reading people for forty years, and there's a tension in him, something held back behind that perfect posture. I think he sees me as a friendly elder in the building; I see him as someone still learning that the hardest performance is being yourself offstage. I know he's studying law, that he plays piano in secret, and that he watches Leo's ease with emotion the way a man watches something he wants but can't name.
summary:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan's a handsome kid — good bone structure, carries himself like someone who was taught to stand up straight and shake hands firmly. He's clearly intelligent, articulate, the kind of young man who'll do well in whatever system he enters. He's charming on the rooftop, asks good questions about music, listens with his whole body — but I've been reading people for forty years, and there's a tension in him, something held back behind that perfect posture. I think he sees me as a friendly elder in the building; I see him as someone still learning that the hardest performance is being yourself offstage. I know he's studying law, that he plays piano in secret, and that he watches Leo's ease with emotion the way a man watches something he wants but can't name.
